question: How can we make an Android app for game emulation, using Game Boy Advance as the example, informed by academic research and open-source projects?
privacy_licensing_posture: Public metadata and public documentation only; no private data, repository clone, package installation, copyrighted ROM, BIOS, or scholarly full-text acquisition.
queries: Android game console emulator architecture
Android retro console emulator development
Game Boy Advance emulator implementation
Game Boy Advance hardware architecture emulation

query_note: queries.txt contains 26 initial variants. The broad all-source run completed 10 and partially processed the eleventh before stopping. queries-pass2.txt contains 15 focused variants covering the remaining technical themes plus an OSS-accuracy refinement; all 15 completed across OpenAlex, Crossref, and arXiv. Official Android documentation covered controller/platform details.
errors: The all-source pass completed 10 of 26 initial query lines, partially processed one more, and recorded 92 connector failures; the remaining technical scope was covered by a successful focused 15-query OpenAlex/Crossref/arXiv pass plus official Android documentation.
Semantic Scholar rate-limited multiple broad-pass queries (HTTP 429).
Several all-preset connectors required credentials or returned HTTP 401/403/404.
OpenAIRE, CORE, and OSTI returned connector schema errors on some queries.
The ARM7TDMI official reference URL returned HTTP 403 to automated retrieval.
The attempted libretro architecture documentation URL returned HTTP 404; the RetroArch README was used instead.
No candidate software was cloned, compiled, benchmarked, or independently accuracy-tested.
No scholarly full text was acquired; claims are bounded to retrieved metadata, abstracts, official docs, and public repository evidence.
